docs.title: Welcome to OpenMIA Developer Docs
docs.text: Build intelligent, multimodal AI applications with the OpenMIA platform.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/openmia/openmia.git
cd openmia

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Run the development server
python main.py

docs.title: 📬 Contact
docs.text: For questions or collaboration, reach out via GitHub Issues.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Complete REST API documentation for the OpenMIA platform.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://api.openmia.ai/v1

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests must include a valid API key in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
GET /health

Response 200 OK

{
  "status": "ok",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uptime": "3d 14h 22m"
}

docs.title: Chat Completion
docs.text:
POST /chat/completions

Request Body

{
  "model": "openmia-v1",
  "messages": [
    { "role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant." },
    { "role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing in one sentence." }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "max_tokens": 256
}

Response 200 OK

{
  "id": "chat-abc123",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "created": 1714000000,
  "model": "openmia-v1",
  "choices": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "message": {
        "role": "assistant",
        "content": "Quantum computing uses qubits to perform many calculations simultaneously."
      },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }
  ],
  "usage": {
    "prompt_tokens": 28,
    "completion_tokens": 12,
    "total_tokens": 40
  }
}

docs.title: Memory — Add
docs.text:
POST /memory

Request Body

{
  "content": "User prefers dark mode.",
  "metadata": {
    "source": "preference",
    "priority": "high"
  }
}

Response 201 Created

{
  "id": "mem-xyz789",
  "status": "stored",
  "created_at": "2026-04-23T08:00:00Z"
}

docs.title: Memory — Search
docs.text:
GET /memory/search?q=dark+mode&limit=5

Response 200 OK

{
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "mem-xyz789",
      "content": "User prefers dark mode.",
      "score": 0.95,
      "created_at": "2026-04-23T08:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Memory — Delete
docs.text:
DELETE /memory/{memory_id}

Response 204 No Content

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 400 Bad Request 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Not Found 429 Rate Limit Exceeded 500 Internal Server Error

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Plan Requests / min Tokens / day Free 10 10,000 Developer 100 500,000 Enterprise Unlimited Unlimited

Need higher limits?

Contact us at support@openmia.ai for enterprise pricing.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: This page describes the high-level architecture of the OpenMIA platform.

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text:
graph TB
    subgraph Client
        A[Web Browser / Mobile App]
    end

    subgraph API Gateway
        B[FastAPI Server]
    end

    subgraph Core Services
        C[Auth Service]
        D[AI Inference Engine]
        E[Memory Manager]
    end

    subgraph Data Layer
        F[(PostgreSQL)]
        G[(Redis Cache)]
        H[(Object Storage)]
    end

    A -->|HTTPS| B
    B --> C
    B --> D
    B --> E
    D --> F
    D --> G
    E --> F
    E --> H

docs.title: API Gateway
docs.text: The FastAPI-based gateway handles all incoming HTTP requests, performs authentication via JWT tokens, and routes requests to the appropriate internal service.

docs.title: AI Inference Engine
docs.text:
The core intelligence layer. It loads and serves transformer-based models for:

  • Natural Language Understanding — intent classification, entity extraction
  • Multimodal Processing — vision + language fusion
  • Memory-Augmented Generation — context-aware response generation

docs.title: Memory Manager
docs.text:
Handles long-term and short-term memory storage:

  • Short-term: Redis-backed session context
  • Long-term: PostgreSQL with vector similarity search

docs.title: Deployment Topology
docs.text:
graph LR
    subgraph Production
        LB[Load Balancer] --> W1[Worker 1]
        LB --> W2[Worker 2]
        LB --> W3[Worker 3]
        W1 --> DB[(Database)]
        W2 --> DB
        W3 --> DB
    end
Component Scaling Strategy API Workers Horizontal (Gunicorn) Inference GPU-bound, vertical Database Primary + Read Replicas Cache Redis Cluster

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• All external communication over TLS 1.3
  • API keys rotated every 90 days
  • Rate limiting: 100 req/min per API key
  • Input sanitization on all endpoints

Production Checklist

Before deploying to production, ensure all environment variables are set and secrets are stored in a secure vault (e.g., GitHub Secrets, HashiCorp Vault).
